title: Consolidate Engine state into single Option<EvaluationState>
analysis:
Engine (reify-eval/src/lib.rs:28-51) has three separate Option fields—current_snapshot, reverse_index, trace_map—that are always set/unset atomically in eval() (lines 931-933) but accessed with individual .expect() calls in edit_param (955-959), prepare_concurrent_edit (231-236), resolve_concurrent_edit (412-415), apply_concurrent_edit (375-376), and check_constraints_with_values (1274-1275). This creates risk of partial-state panics and obscures the invariant that these fields are always Some simultaneously after eval().

The fix consolidates these into `eval_state: Option<EvaluationState>` and introduces an `EngineError` enum so methods that require prior eval() return `Result` instead of panicking. This changes return types of edit_param, prepare_concurrent_edit, resolve_concurrent_edit, apply_concurrent_edit, check_constraints_with_values, and edit_check—affecting ~40+ test call sites across reify-eval/tests/ and reify-runtime/tests/concurrent_eval.rs, plus reify-runtime/src/concurrent_eval.rs.

Additionally, lines 680-741 (let-binding evaluation in eval()) and 856-915 (let-binding re-evaluation after resolution in eval()) contain nearly identical code: collect let cells with expressions, build traces, topological sort, evaluate in order with journal/cache recording. This should be extracted into a shared helper.

Methods that gracefully handle None (check_snapshot at line 1637, build_snapshot at line 1742, snapshot() at line 205) use `as_ref()?` or `as_ref()` and can be trivially updated to access eval_state.snapshot instead.

The two usages of `if let Some(rev_idx) = &self.reverse_index` at lines 1017 and 1074 inside edit_param are within a scope that already extracted reverse_index at the top, so after refactoring they'll use the already-extracted reference directly.

prerequisites.description: Define EngineError enum with NotInitialized variant and EvaluationState struct containing snapshot: Snapshot, reverse_index: ReverseDependencyIndex, trace_map: HashMap<NodeId, DependencyTrace>. Add EngineError to lib.rs public API. Use #[derive(Debug)] on both. Implement std::fmt::Display and std::error::Error for EngineError.

steps.description: Write test in crates/reify-eval/tests/boundary5_engine.rs: `engine_is_not_initialized_before_eval` — Engine::new() then assert !engine.is_initialized(). Write second test `engine_is_initialized_after_eval` — eval(&module) then assert engine.is_initialized().

Replace the three Option fields (current_snapshot, reverse_index, trace_map) with `eval_state: Option<EvaluationState>` in Engine struct. Add `pub fn is_initialized(&self) -> bool { self.eval_state.is_some() }`. Update Engine::new() to set eval_state: None. Update eval() to store `self.eval_state = Some(EvaluationState { snapshot, reverse_index, trace_map })` at line 931-933. Update snapshot() accessor to `self.eval_state.as_ref().map(|s| &s.snapshot)`. Update check_snapshot (line 1637) and build_snapshot (line 1742) to use `self.eval_state.as_ref()?.snapshot`.

Write test `edit_param_before_eval_returns_error` in boundary5_engine.rs: Engine::new() then call edit_param() and assert it returns Err(EngineError::NotInitialized). This tests that the error is clear, not a panic.

Change edit_param return type from EvalResult to Result<EvalResult, EngineError>. Replace the three .expect() calls at lines 955-959 with a single `let state = self.eval_state.as_ref().ok_or(EngineError::NotInitialized)?;` and extract snapshot, reverse_index, trace_map from state. Update the two `if let Some(rev_idx) = &self.reverse_index` patterns at lines 1017 and 1074 to use the already-extracted reverse_index reference directly. Update the final state store at line 1247 to mutate `self.eval_state.as_mut().unwrap().snapshot = new_snapshot`. Wrap successful return in Ok().

Write test `eval_state_available_atomically_after_eval` in boundary5_engine.rs: After eval(), verify that engine.is_initialized() is true, engine.snapshot() is Some, and the snapshot contains the expected graph, reverse_index, and trace_map data (accessible via a new eval_state() accessor). This proves all three components are available as a single atomic unit.

Add `pub fn eval_state(&self) -> Option<&EvaluationState>` accessor to Engine. Update edit_check to propagate the Result from edit_param (change return type to Result<CheckResult, EngineError>). Verify all existing tests in boundary5_engine.rs and incremental.rs still compile and pass after updating their edit_param/edit_check call sites to unwrap the Result (.unwrap() for tests that expect success).

Update prepare_concurrent_edit to return Result<ConcurrentEditSetup, EngineError>: replace three .expect() calls at lines 231-236 with single eval_state access. Update resolve_concurrent_edit: replace .expect() calls at lines 412-415 with eval_state access, change return type to include Result or use unwrap since it's always called after prepare. Update apply_concurrent_edit: replace .expect() at line 376 with eval_state access. Update check_constraints_with_values to return Result<(Vec<ConstraintCheckEntry>, Vec<Diagnostic>), EngineError>: replace .expect() at line 1275 with eval_state access.

Update all test callers in crates/reify-eval/tests/ to handle Result return types: add .unwrap() to edit_param calls in incremental.rs (~30 sites), concurrent.rs (~5 sites), journal.rs (~1 site), resolution.rs (~1 site), interactive_edit_loop.rs (~4 sites). Add .unwrap() to prepare_concurrent_edit calls in concurrent.rs (~3 sites). Add .unwrap() to check_constraints_with_values calls where applicable. Ensure all tests compile and pass.

Update crates/reify-runtime/src/concurrent_eval.rs: edit_param_concurrent (line 271) — handle Result from prepare_concurrent_edit by mapping EngineError into SchedulerError (add EngineError variant or convert). Update resolve_concurrent_edit call (line 297) and apply_concurrent_edit call (line 335). Update check_constraints_with_values call (line 339) in edit_check_concurrent. Update crates/reify-runtime/tests/concurrent_eval.rs call sites (~8 sites) to handle new Result types.

Write test `let_binding_evaluation_produces_same_results_with_helper` in boundary5_engine.rs: Create a module with multiple let bindings that have forward references (e.g., let a = b + 1; let b = param * 2). Eval and verify all let binding values are correct, including after resolution phase. This serves as a regression test for the helper extraction in step-11.

Extract duplicate let-binding evaluation code (lines 680-741 and 856-915 in eval()) into a private helper method `evaluate_let_bindings(&mut self, template: &CompiledTopologyTemplate, values: &mut ValueMap, snapshot: &mut Snapshot, version_id: u64)`. The helper collects let cells with default_expr, builds NodeId keys and DependencyTrace maps, topological sorts, evaluates each in order via reify_expr::eval_expr, updates values/snapshot.values, records cache entry via self.cache.record_evaluation, and records Started/Completed journal events. Replace both call sites (lines 680-741 and 856-915) with calls to the helper. Verify all existing tests pass.

design_decisions.decision: Use Result<T, EngineError> return types instead of keeping panicking .expect() calls
design_decisions.rationale: Task explicitly requires 'clear error (not panic)' for edit_param before eval. The ok_or pattern is specified in the task description. While this has large blast radius (~40+ call sites), it eliminates partial-state panic risk and provides proper error propagation.

design_decisions.decision: Keep check_snapshot and build_snapshot returning Option (not Result)
design_decisions.rationale: These methods already handle the uninitialized case gracefully by returning None via the ? operator. Changing them to Result would be unnecessary churn since their callers already expect Option.

design_decisions.decision: Use eval_state.as_mut().unwrap() for mutations within edit_param after the initial Result check
design_decisions.rationale: Once we've validated eval_state is Some at the top of edit_param (via ok_or), subsequent mutable access within the same method can safely unwrap. The borrow checker prevents holding both a shared and mutable reference, so we validate once then use unwrap for mutation points.

design_decisions.decision: Helper method for let-binding evaluation takes &mut self to access journal and cache
design_decisions.rationale: Both duplicate code blocks record journal events and cache entries via self.journal and self.cache. The helper needs mutable access to Engine, and also needs the template, values, snapshot, and version_id as parameters.

design_decisions.decision: Update test callers with .unwrap() rather than expect() with messages
design_decisions.rationale: Tests that call edit_param on an already-evaluated engine should never fail the initialization check. Using .unwrap() is idiomatic for test code and keeps the diff minimal. The panic message from unwrap on EngineError::NotInitialized is sufficiently descriptive.

design_decisions.decision: Consolidate resolve_concurrent_edit to use unwrap rather than Result for eval_state access
design_decisions.rationale: resolve_concurrent_edit is always called after prepare_concurrent_edit succeeds, which already validated eval_state is Some. Adding Result return type here would be unnecessary ceremony since the precondition is structurally guaranteed by the call sequence in both sequential and concurrent paths.

reuse.how: Use existing test fixtures and builders for new tests, following the pattern already established in boundary5_engine.rs and incremental.rs
These already produce the three components that will become EvaluationState fields — bundle their results into the new struct
Already used in both duplicate code blocks — the helper will call it once
Already used in both duplicate blocks for building traces and recording cache entries

reuse.what: Existing test patterns in incremental.rs for edit_param verification
reuse.how: Follow the same engine setup pattern: bracket_compiled_module() + MockConstraintChecker::new() + Engine::new() + eval() before testing
